---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Executive Summary {.unnumbered}

This report is a `r report_status` for the `r assessment_name` in the
`r assessment_area`. The draft follows the structure of the previous bigeye
tuna assessment while separating text that is broadly reusable from statements
that must be regenerated from the `r assessment_year` model outputs.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment described an integrated
MULTIFAN-CL assessment of bigeye tuna in the western and central Pacific Ocean
with major supporting analyses for abundance indices, tag reporting and tagger
effects, size composition preparation, length-weight conversion factors, and
MFCL software developments [@teears_cpue_2023; @peatman_analysis_2023;
@peatman_analysis_2023-1; @peatman_analysis_2023-2;
@macdonald_project_2023-1; @davies_developments_2023]. Those components form
the starting point for this `r assessment_year` draft, but all year-specific
model settings, final data years, diagnostics, and management-advice quantities
must be checked against the current analysis.

As a benchmark for comparison, the `r previous_assessment_year` report
estimated median recent spawning depletion at 0.35 for 2018-2021 after
combining structural and estimation uncertainty, with an 80 percent interval of
0.30-0.40 and a full range of 0.25-0.46. Median recent fishing mortality
relative to $F_{MSY}$ was 0.59 for 2017-2020, with an 80 percent interval of
0.46-0.74 and a full range of 0.37-0.99. The `r previous_assessment_year`
assessment concluded that the WCPO bigeye tuna stock was not overfished and
was not undergoing overfishing, and that no model in the uncertainty grid,
including estimation uncertainty, was below the 20 percent $SB_{F=0}$ limit
reference point.

Those values are included here only as the previous-assessment baseline. The
`r assessment_year` executive summary should replace them with current
diagnostic-model, structural-uncertainty, and estimation-uncertainty results
once the final model set has been accepted.

## Main Updates Since `r previous_assessment_year`

> **TODO:** Summarise only changes confirmed for the `r assessment_year`
> assessment, including the final data years, input-preparation changes, CPUE
> standardisation changes, tagging changes, biological assumptions, MFCL or
> mfclrtmb changes, and convergence or uncertainty treatment.

The main changes that should be checked first against the `r assessment_year`
workflow are the same areas that materially changed the `r previous_assessment_year`
assessment relative to 2020: the catch-conditioned model framework and index
fishery CPUE likelihood; sdmTMB-based standardised CPUE inputs; index-fishery
CPUE variance treatment; internally estimated natural mortality using a
Lorenzen form; internally estimated growth using conditional age-at-length
data; revised tagger-effect and tag-reporting treatment; size-composition
filtering and weighting; reduced use of non-decreasing selectivity penalties;
and the combined treatment of structural and estimation uncertainty.

## Stock Status Summary

> **TODO:** Insert final recent depletion, fishing mortality, uncertainty
> ranges, limit-reference-point status, and harvest-strategy objective status
> only after the management-advice model set is finalised.

## Key Diagnostics and Uncertainty

> **TODO:** Summarise convergence, Hessian and jitter outcomes, major
> sensitivities, and the axes that remain in the structural uncertainty grid.

## Main Conclusions

> **TODO:** Replace this list with final assessment conclusions. Do not reuse
> the `r previous_assessment_year` stock-status conclusions unless each
> conclusion has been checked against the `r assessment_year` model outputs.
